Now speaking

The written briefing

World

  • US–Iran escalation. The US military says it struck ~140 targets inside Iran overnight; Iran says it fired back toward Jordan and other Gulf states — the sharpest exchange yet. PressTV · NPR
  • Sen. Lindsey Graham has died of a ruptured aorta, shortly after returning from Ukraine. WORLD
  • World Cup. Argentina reached the semis on a late Álvarez strike; Spain face France on Tuesday in Texas, both unbeaten. NPR
  • Science: heavy repeated practice physically rewires the brain, letting a trained skill bypass the prefrontal cortex for dedicated circuits. ScienceDaily

AI & Tech

  • GPT-5.6 went live Jul 9 and is now the default in ChatGPT (coding & reasoning gains). OpenAI
  • Gemini 3.1 Pro is leading the hardest reasoning benchmarks (ARC-AGI-2). Fello AI
  • Claude Sonnet 5 shipped Jun 30 as Anthropic's most agentic Sonnet yet. BuildFastWithAI

Weather — Athens

  • Hot, mostly sunny, northerly winds. Low 30s in the morning, peaking near 36°C in the afternoon, high 20s by evening. No real rain risk for Athens. Sunset ~20:45. Athens Times

Personal & Communication

  • Calendar connector unavailable today — check it manually.
  • 2 account-security login alerts to verify (new devices). Nothing else urgent in the inbox.

Climbing — Chamonix Lead & Speed

  • Men's lead: Alberto Ginés López wins his first-ever World Cup gold, over Luka Potočar. Lacrux
  • Women's lead: Annie Sanders (18) takes her third gold of the season; Chaehyun Seo on the podium again.
  • Speed: Olympic champ Veddriq Leonardo returns from injury to win in 4.89s. Newsy Today

Gaming & Deals

  • Epic free games (until Jul 16, ~18:00 Athens): Nova Lands (open-world factory builder, normally $19.99) and Tattoo Tycoon ($29.99). Yardbarker

Learning

  • Word of the day — crux: the hardest, most decisive point of a climb or a problem. "The third clip was the crux; once I stuck it, the rest flowed."
  • AI tip: put your instructions before the long text you want a model to act on — models follow the ask more reliably when it comes first.
